⫘⫘⫘⫘⫘⫘

𝐈𝐍𝐓𝐑𝐎

It was supposed to be a chill day. Hanna’s birthday. Gyuri had agreed to take her out to that massive city mall, the one with the cinema, arcades, and a garden big enough to feel like a forest. Hanna said there was a "small cosplay event." Which, obviously, was code for full-scale anime convention packed with sweaty nerds and sword-wielding weirdos.

Gyuri already hated it.

"I swear to God, Hanna. If I see one more guy with cat ears, I’m throwing hands."

“Come oooon~ it’s my birthdayyy~,” Hanna giggled, tugging her arm “I got a surprise for you~”

“That tone. That’s a dangerous-ass tone.”

They reached the dressing booth near the restroom area when Hanna pulled out the surprise. A black-and-white maid outfit.

With frills

And thigh-highs

Gyuri’s soul left her body

“You’re joking.” Gyuri stared at the outfit like it was cursed

“Do it for meee~ Just one hour! Just a walk around the con floor and then we get bubble tea, I swear! Pleaaaase, Gyu-Gyu!”

“Don’t call me that.”

“Gyu-Gyu. Pleaaaase~”

With a dramatic groan and the dead-eyed walk of someone attending their own funeral, Gyuri took the costume and stormed off to the restroom

⫘⫘⫘⫘⫘⫘

𝐁𝐀𝐂𝐊𝐆𝐑𝐎𝐔𝐍𝐃

Gyuri was 21, a college student, fast, athletic, and pretty sharp when she wasn’t pissed off. But if there was one thing that annoyed her more than rude people and loud chewers, it was being embarrassed. Unfortunately for her, she had curves that most girls would die for. Sexy body? Check. Big Booooobieees, Big hips, tight waist, All of it, which she hated. It made her feel like people didn’t take her seriously, especially at the gym, where dudes treated her like some kind of walking thirst trap instead of a legit athlete.

She had a soft spot though. That soft spot was named Hanna, her childhood best friend for ten years. Hanna was everything Gyuri wasn’t. Bubbly, annoying in the cutest way possible, and a total chaos goblin who lived to tease Gyuri into emotional combustion. But Hanna had a heart condition....literally. And Gyuri was the one always holding her hand in hospital waiting rooms, always watching her meds like a hawk, and pretending not to cry when Hanna would pass out from overexertion. They were ride or die
